summary: PR 2340 is a one-line fighter decomp fix in Kirby's ftKb_SpecialNZd.c. In ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll, the landing/collision branch now calls the common landing transition ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic(gobj) instead of ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105E8C(gobj) after clearing two Kirby state fields. The bot report says this made ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll a 100% match for +84 bytes, but the loaded report also shows unrelated ef unit match churn/regressions, so only the local Kirby function result should be treated as grounded for this PR.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; updates ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll helper call in the landing collision branch.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll hunk replaces ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105E8C(gobj) with ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic(gobj)

what_changed.text: In ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll, when ft_80081D0C(gobj) is true, the code still clears fp->fv.kb.xB4 and fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x4, but now enters ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic(gobj) rather than ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105E8C(gobj).
The changed-file metadata shows this PR touched only ftKb_SpecialNZd.c with one added and one deleted line.
The automated report records ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd as improving from 0.00% to 100.00%, +84 bytes.

smart_moves.text: A minimal helper substitution was used instead of wider restructuring: the PR only changed the transition call needed for the target function.
The fix reused an existing common fighter landing helper, ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic, rather than introducing local control flow or a new wrapper.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For collision callbacks that detect landing with ft_80081D0C and then clear state, check whether the original source used a common ftCo landing transition helper rather than a character-specific special-action helper.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot report: ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll +84 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Try semantically appropriate shared transition helpers when a nearly matched action/collision callback differs only in the called transition routine.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The slice does not include assembly or objdiff details beyond the bot report, so this should stay a narrow tactic rather than a global rule.
